meta_title: For Freelancers: idea validation to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Idea Validation to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to validate ideas before building and use Antigravity and Claude Code to ship bug-free projects fast.
content.answer_block: When you can't debug code, you're likely building the wrong thing or using the wrong stack. Stop coding blindly. Validate your idea first using high-level logic frameworks. Then, leverage AI agents like Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, ensuring your technical foundation is sound before you commit to production.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and the console screams a wall of red text. You’ve been staring at the same bug for three hours, and your client’s deadline is looming like a thunderstorm. You feel the familiar, crushing weight of imposter syndrome creeping in—the fear that maybe you aren't cut out for this. 

But here is the secret the senior devs won't tell you: the best way to solve a bug is to never write the buggy code in the first place. By shifting your focus from 'hacking it together' to 'rigorous idea validation,' you turn the development process from a chaotic scramble into a precise, predictable execution. Let's optimize your workflow.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Before writing a single line of logic, force your idea through a validation filter to ensure the scope is manageable and the technical requirements are clear.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project idea: [Insert Idea]. Break it down into core functional requirements. Identify the 20% of features that provide 80% of the value. Flag any potential technical bottlenecks that could lead to complex debugging sessions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --ask 'Generate a technical requirements document for [Project Name] focusing on simplicity and maintainability.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: Most bugs stem from poor data structures. Validate your schema first to prevent logic errors later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for [Project Name]. Include key entities and relationships. Ensure the structure minimizes data redundancy and potential null-reference errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --ask 'Create a Prisma schema for the database structure defined in the project docs.'

steps.title: Automated Logic Testing
steps.description: Use AI to write the tests before the implementation. This forces you to think about edge cases before they become bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of unit tests for the core logic of [Module Name]. Cover success, failure, and edge-case scenarios to ensure robust error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --ask 'Generate Jest unit tests for the existing logic in src/lib/core.ts.'

steps.title: Iterative Code Generation
steps.description: Build in small, testable chunks. Claude Code allows you to generate code that is purpose-built to pass your existing test suite.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current implementation of [Feature Name] to be more modular. Ensure the code is self-documenting and follows clean-code principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Implement the auth module following the architecture specs and ensure all tests pass.'

steps.title: Automated Debugging Session
steps.description: When an error inevitably occurs, use the AI agent to trace the root cause rather than guessing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following error stack trace: [Paste Trace]. Identify the root cause and provide a fix that prevents regression.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --ask 'Analyze the logs in the console and propose a fix for the current runtime error.'

pro_tips: Always treat your prompt as a system instruction: define the persona (e.g., 'Senior Staff Engineer') to increase code quality.
Use 'dry-run' modes in your CLI tools to see the code structure before applying changes to your filesystem.
If you're stuck, delete the module and re-generate; often, the AI's first attempt at a clean slate is better than a manual patch job.
Document your validation process in a README.md; clients pay more when they see a structured engineering methodology.
Prioritize 'Fail Fast' logic; add explicit error handling at the start of functions to avoid deep-nested debugging.
content.next_steps: Once you have validated and built your MVP, deploy to a staging environment and run a smoke test before showing the client. Iterate based on feedback using the same AI-driven validation loop.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start architecting your success. Our community of elite builders is waiting to help you scale your freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
